method.name: Generative Adversarial NAS (GA-NAS)
method.description: A provably converging and efficient search algorithm for Neural Architecture Search based on adversarial learning
method.inspiration: Cross Entropy (CE) method in importance sampling
components: Architecture generator network based on RNN and GNN
Discriminator to distinguish winning architectures from randomly generated ones
method.key_innovation: Does not obtain rewards by evaluating generated architectures, but learns a discriminator instead
method.theoretical_foundation: Connects GA-NAS to an importance sampling method with a symmetric Jensen-Shannon (JS) divergence loss
method.convergence_property: Provably converging in a finite number of steps

evaluation_criteria: Reproducibility and stability with random seeds

results.performance: Outperforms existing NAS algorithms including EA, RL, BO, DARTS
results.achievements: State-of-the-art results on three representative NAS benchmark sets
